prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
Here are two translations that capture the spirit of the proverb in different languages:

French:
"Il aurait fallu planter l'arbre il y a vingt ans. Le moment idéal pour le faire maintenant est arrivé."

Note: This French translation elegantly conveys the idea that now is the perfect time to take action, even if starting twenty years ago would have been ideal.

Spanish:
"El mejor momento para sembrar un árbol fue hace veinte años. Pero este momento también es excelente para hacerlo."

Note: The Spanish version emphasizes that while twenty years ago was the best time to plant the tree, the current time is also an excellent opportunity to do so, capturing the optimistic essence of the original proverb.
